message:
fix: Use @TypeOf() for unknown captured variable types in closures

When a nested function captures a variable from an outer scope, the type
lookup may fail due to scope mismatch (e.g., inner function capturing
outer function's typed parameter). Instead of falling back to
*runtime.PyObject, use @TypeOf(var_name) to let Zig infer the actual
type at capture time.

This fixes the capture type for test_simple_closure.py (though there's
a separate issue with closure return types that needs further work).
